input.background: The Wechsler Adult Intelligence Scale (WAIS) is a commonly used intelligence test that is calibrated to produce a Normal distribution of scores with μ=100 and σ=15 for various age groups. Based on the 68-95-99.7 rule, we can say that:
input.question: What percentage of the scores lie in the range 100 ± 15?

output.answer: 68% of the scores lie in the range 100 ± 15 = 85 to 115.

mean_score.description: The mean score of the WAIS test, which is the average score expected in the distribution.

standard_deviation.description: The standard deviation of the WAIS test scores, indicating the spread of scores around the mean.

score_range.description: The range of scores within one standard deviation from the mean (100 ± 15).
